layman_headline: Fear grips the crypto market — patience beats impulse right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a fearful mood with most altcoins in downtrends and Bitcoin dominating capital flows. Every swing candidate on our watchlist has a bearish chart structure, sitting below key moving averages. While some coins like ETH and PEPE are approaching oversold territory, none have confirmed a reversal yet. Jumping in now would be catching a falling knife rather than buying a proven bounce.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. Set price alerts on ETH around $1,800 support and watch for a confirmed bounce with rising volume before committing capital.
expert_reasoning: All nine swing candidates present bearish chart structures — every single one is below key moving averages. The Fear & Greed index at 28 suggests contrarian opportunity is building but hasn't matured. BTC dominance at 56.5% continues to pressure altcoins. ETH has the best risk profile with RSI at 39, $1,800 support nearby (-2.2%), and $41B in ecosystem TVL providing fundamental backing. However, the chart bias score is -0.70 (bearish), the risk/reward to nearest levels is only 1.41x, and there's no MACD bullish cross or volume breakout confirmation. The prudent play is to wait for ETH to test and hold $1,800 support with a bullish reversal candle before entering.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.5% and rising continues to siphon capital from altcoins — ETH could break below $1,800 support
Fear & Greed at 28 could deteriorate further toward extreme fear (sub-20), dragging all assets lower
Tether USDT regulatory countdown (CoinDesk) and GENIUS Act deadline miss create stablecoin uncertainty that could reduce market liquidity
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,800 support — a bounce with volume confirms entry; a break below invalidates the setup
Fear & Greed index movement — a spike above 35 would signal improving sentiment and better entry conditions
BTC dominance trend — any reversal below 56% would be the green light for alt positioning
market_context: The crypto market is in a fear phase (28/100) with BTC flat at $64.4K and high dominance at 56.5%, crushing altcoin performance. All swing candidates show bearish chart structures, suggesting we're in a bottoming process that hasn't completed.
context_banner: Market fear is building toward contrarian opportunity, but no confirmed reversal signals yet — disciplined patience is the edge.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39 approaching oversold territory, our most reliable signal source (80% win rate), but not yet at the sub-30 extreme that historically produces the best bounce entries.
Market fear at 28/100 with flat trend suggests contrarian buying opportunity is developing but sentiment hasn't capitulated enough for a high-conviction bottom call.
US agencies missing GENIUS Act stablecoin deadline creates regulatory uncertainty that could weigh on market liquidity and sentiment near-term.
ETH derivatives reset noted by Blockworks suggests positioning has been cleaned out, which could enable a stronger move when a catalyst arrives.
Every swing candidate shows price below all major moving averages with bearish chart bias scores (-0.45 to -0.70), indicating broad market weakness with no confirmed reversals.
